skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Guides agents through a written implementation plan using critical review, task batches, verification, and feedback checkpoints.
skill.summary: Execute written implementation plans in controlled batches with review checkpoints.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static finding for weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line is a natural language description about executing plans, not cryptographic code or an unsafe algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3, but this line only describes plan execution with review checkpoints. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains plain metadata text and no code path. There is no evidence of cryptographic behavior or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Implementation plans can drift when work is done in one long pass. This skill keeps execution structured with batch work, verification, and feedback checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Loads and reviews a written implementation plan before work begins.
Identifies concerns or gaps before starting execution.
Executes plan tasks in batches, with a default first batch of three tasks.
Tracks task progress from in progress to completed.
Runs the verifications specified by the plan.
Reports completed work and verification results before continuing.
limitations: Requires a written implementation plan to guide the work.
Does not create the original plan from scratch.
Stops for clarification when instructions are unclear or blockers appear.
References a finishing skill that must be available for final branch completion.

use_cases.title: Execute a Prepared Development Plan
use_cases.description: Work through an approved plan in small task batches with verification after each batch.

use_cases.title: Maintain Review Control
use_cases.description: Pause between batches so an architect or lead can inspect progress before more changes are made.

use_cases.title: Recover from Blocked Implementation
use_cases.description: Stop execution when instructions fail, tests break repeatedly, or dependencies are missing.

prompt_templates.title: Run This Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to implement the plan in plans/feature-plan.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear plan file.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Executing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill. First review this plan for gaps, then start the first batch only if it is ready.
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan needs a careful check before changes start.

prompt_templates.title: Batch with Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to execute the first three tasks. Run each specified verification and report results before continuing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants controlled progress and test evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Resume from Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to apply my feedback, revisit the plan if needed, then continue with the next batch.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after a review checkpoint.

output_examples.input: Execute the migration plan and stop after the first batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, completes the first three tasks, runs verification, summarizes changes, and waits for feedback.
The assistant applies requested changes, updates task status, starts the next batch, and reports verification results.

output_examples.input: A verification failed twice during the batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops execution, explains the blocker, lists the failed verification, and asks for clarification.

best_practices: Provide a complete plan file with small, ordered tasks.
Include explicit verification commands or review criteria in the plan.
Review each checkpoint before asking the assistant to continue.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without a written implementation plan.
Asking the assistant to skip verification steps from the plan.
Continuing through repeated failures instead of stopping for clarification.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant execute a written implementation plan in batches with review checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it write the implementation plan?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute an existing plan, not create the first draft.

faq.question: How many tasks does it run first?
faq.answer: The default first batch is three tasks unless the plan or user requests a different batch.

faq.question: When does it stop?
faq.answer: It stops when there is a blocker, unclear instruction, repeated verification failure, or critical plan gap.

faq.question: Can it handle user feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It applies feedback, revisits the plan when needed, and continues with the next batch.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
